ORDER RE: STIPULATED EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR A TRIAL CONTINUANCE

DISCOVERY 610, TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. DEFENDANT EC HOME IMPROVEMENTS INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION TO PLAINTIFF, SET TWO, IS DENIED AS THE REQUESTS ARE OVERLY BROAD, INVADE THE RIGHT TO PRIVACY, AND ARE NOT CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE AND RELEVANT EVIDENCE. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT. NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF VICTORIA AUSTIN'S Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is Granted, no opposition filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-19-2010 Continued To Jun-23-10 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production To Pltf, Set Two, And For Sanctions; Memorandum Of P/A; And Declarationof Reed; Statement Of Requests For Production And Responses In Dispute
Off Calendar Apr-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Christine B. Reed, Esq., for defendant and moving party: EC HOME IMPROVEMENTS INC.
Scott Isherwood, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Victoria Austin

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT EC HOME IMPROVEMENTS INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production To Plaintiff, Set Two, is denied as the requests are overly broad, invade the right to privacy, and are not calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible and relevant evidence. All requests for sanctions are
denied. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.
